facts: The atmosphere on Venus is 92 times thicker than Earth's, with pressures similar to being 1 mile underwater
The surface temperature on Venus reaches 870°F, hot enough to melt lead, which is equivalent to the heat of 6,000 football fields filled with burning cars

A day on Venus is 243 Earth days long, which is even longer than its year, at 225 Earth days, making it a very slow rotator, with a rotation period comparable to the distance from New York to Los Angeles
idea.payoff: and we still don't know why its rotation is so slow
idea.title: Venus: Toxic Paradise 🌋
idea.description: Imagine a world where the atmosphere is crushingly thick, and the heat is so intense it could melt metal 🌡️. That's Venus, our neighboring planet, with conditions so extreme, it's hard to wrap your head around them 🤯. But what's even more fascinating is its bizarre rotation - and the mystery behind it is still unsolved 🌌

scenes.visual: Ominous, cloudy orange/yellow Venus filling the screen, slowly rotating.

scenes.text: Atmosphere 92x thicker than Earth's. Pressures like 1 mile underwater!
scenes.visual: Venus with layers of atmosphere visible, a pressure gauge icon appears and spikes, then fades into an underwater visual effect.

scenes.text: Surface: 870°F! Hot enough to melt lead. (Heat of 6,000 burning car fields)
scenes.visual: Fiery, volcanic landscape of Venus. Molten lead visual effect pouring across the screen. A thermometer icon spikes to extreme heat.

scenes.text: A Venusian day is 243 Earth days. Longer than its 225-day year!
scenes.visual: Venus slowly rotating against a starfield. A smaller Earth next to it rotates rapidly in comparison. A clock icon animates a slow rotation.

scenes.text: We STILL don't know why its rotation is so slow!
scenes.visual: Venus slowly spinning, a large, stylized question mark appears and pulses over the planet's surface.
